pi_items.metadata_value: NCH Copper Point of Sale used in task execution
pi_items.verified_value: NCH Copper Point of Sale v3.06 (July 2018) from NCH Software, Australian company. Free for non-commercial use. Uses SQLite database.
pi_items.source: env.json description, env_creation_notes/specific_env_notes/copper_point_of_sale_env/notes.md, NCH Software official website (nchsoftware.com)

pi_items.verified_value: Item appears to be synthetic/fabricated for this task. The specific code 'VR01' and name 'Virus Removal' combination do not appear in any public service database. Price $89.00 is plausible for real virus removal services (GimmiBYTE charges $80 flat rate + hourly; Best Buy/Geek Squad charges $99.99-$199.99), but this exact code/price combination is not found in any authoritative source.
pi_items.source: Web search for 'Virus Removal' service pricing; NCH Copper environment is documented as using synthetic test data (env_creation_notes)

pi_items.verified_value: Item appears to be synthetic. The specific code 'ST01' does not appear in public sources. However, the price $49.99 for 'System Tune-up' matches Norton's actual Computer Tune Up service pricing ($49.99 one-time service). The name and price are plausible but the code is fabricated.
pi_items.source: Web search for 'System Tune-up' service pricing; Norton Computer Tune Up confirmed at $49.99; env_creation_notes confirm synthetic data environment

pi_items.verified_value: Item appears to be synthetic. The specific code 'DT01' and service 'Data Transfer' at $60.00 do not appear in any public service catalog or pricing database. The price is plausible for data transfer services but cannot be verified.
pi_items.source: Web search for 'Data Transfer' service pricing; env_creation_notes confirm synthetic data environment

pi_items.verified_value: Database paths are plausible Windows installation paths for NCH Copper using standard Windows data directories (ProgramData and AppData). The specific paths match the pattern documented in env_creation_notes showing Copper installation at 'C:\Program Files (x86)\NCH Software\Copper\copper.exe'. However, exact database location verification requires direct software execution.
pi_items.source: env.json, install_copper.ps1 shows installation pattern; env_creation_notes/specific_env_notes/copper_point_of_sale_env/notes.md documents Copper paths; Windows AppData/ProgramData documentation

pi_items.verified_value: CRITICAL BUG FOUND: Line 63 of verifier.py has a typo: 'if notdb_copied:' should be 'if not db_copied:' (missing space). This is a Python syntax error that causes NameError when database copy fails. This bug prevents proper verification of the task.
pi_items.source: Direct code analysis of verifier.py in create_service_items task; Confirmed by execution logs showing 'verifier error: name notdb_copied is not defined' in artifact episode_20260306_231843_71a5064d-8c9e-42e7-bcec-b6b4cc0c3b6c/summary.json

pi_items.verified_value: Synthetic. Environment uses synthetic product data from Shopify Partners product-csvs (100 items in products.csv). Task items (Virus Removal, System Tune-up, Data Transfer) are not from external datasets but fabricated specifically for this task.
pi_items.source: env_creation_notes/specific_env_notes/copper_point_of_sale_env/notes.md (line 111) documents 'Shopify Partners product-csvs' as source; products.csv analysis shows Shopify sample data; add_customer task validated_pi.json confirms synthetic nature of environment data

privileged_info_summary: The 'create_service_items' task uses NCH Copper Point of Sale v3.06 (verified real software from NCH Software, free for non-commercial use). The task requires creating three service items with synthetic codes and names (VR01, ST01, DT01) that do not appear in any public database. Item prices are plausible ($89 for virus removal, $49.99 for system tune-up matching Norton's real price, $60 for data transfer) but cannot be verified as authoritative values. Database paths referenced are plausible Windows locations but exact paths cannot be verified without software execution. CRITICAL ISSUE: The verifier.py code contains a bug on line 63 (typo 'notdb_copied' instead of 'not db_copied') that causes the verifier to crash with NameError when database copy fails, preventing proper task verification. The task executes within a synthetic environment documented to use Shopify Partners sample product data.
